reviews.author: Darren Andrews
reviews.text: A fascinating place to visit and learn about the history of the hospital and Vietnam / American war (as the locals refer to it). Recommend visiting independently and in the afternoon as the morning is very crammed with endless boat excursions all squeezing in during the morning. Fairly warm inside the caves. Prepare to climb 100 steps up and down (see photos). Nearby toilets available for free and reasonably clean. Overall worth a visit if you can visit at a quieter time.

reviews.author: Irene Krajewska
reviews.text: Well worth a visit. Amazing huge limestone cave with three storeys of concealed concreted tunnels and rooms used in the war. Fascinating history. The entrance is a short but steep stepped climb to access. Nice views of the nearby countryside from the elevated entrance.

reviews.author: R Jun
reviews.text: The Military Hospital Cave is a fascinating stop during a Ha Long Bay cruise, offering a glimpse into Vietnam’s wartime history hidden within limestone cliffs. Used as a secret hospital during the Vietnam War, the cave still holds treatment rooms, operating areas and command spaces. Walking through it feels surreal—where natural beauty quietly conceals stories of resilience and survival.

reviews.author: dom coleman
reviews.text: This was a great visit and a valuable bit of historic knowledge about the area. There is a small admission fee and when you get to the cave, there are locals that will guide you round and introduce you to the history, without one of these people showing you round and sharing the historic knowledge, the effect would be much less. They don't expect you to pay them but I felt a tip was definitely justified for their time. Definitely worth a visit.

reviews.author: Michael Tsafrir
reviews.text: Interesting experience. The place outside is beautiful, the drive there is stunning. Enterance fee is 50,000 VND. You go up stairs to the cave, and then enter the bunker, where the vietnam military had a hospital field camp. It is very interesting to see, you get the feeling how it was there for the soldiers, and go through the bunker outside again.

reviews.author: Music R Us
reviews.text: Really cool cave if you like your history about the vietnam war, plenty of small rooms with little models and trinkets from wartime, would definitely recommend if your looking for something to do on the island, an awesome way to spend an couple of hours. 50k vnd for tickets so nice and cheap aswell 😁🤘

reviews.author: laura palazzetti
reviews.text: Visiting the Hospital Cave on Cát Bà Island was an unforgettable experience! The ticket includes a guided tour, though it isn’t strictly necessary as the rooms are quite self-explanatory thanks to the mannequins and detailed displays. Still, we had the greatest pleasure of being guided by Cai, and he elevated the visit to a whole new level. Cai walked us through every room with care, explaining their purpose and adding fascinating historical insights that brought the entire cave hospital to life. His knowledge and passion made the tour feel immersive rather than just informative. He was also incredibly kind, even offering to take a few photos of my partner and me, such a thoughtful touch! If you’re on Cát Bà, take the time to visit this site, you won’t regret it. And if you arrive by motorbike, there are plenty of places to park right in front of the ticket office.

reviews.author: Michael Redding
reviews.text: Deeply sobering. The tenacity, the drive, the dedication to overcoming insurmountable odds is overwhelmingly on display here. Our guide was amazing, showing us each room and explaining the history and conditions in which these doctors worked. This place holds such intense natural beauty and man-made strife. Worth every penny to see.

reviews.author: Jude Jackson
reviews.text: Great insite to what happened in the war many years ago. Not very big as the 3rd floor had a rock collapse. 50,000 to get in free parking and a cafe opposite

reviews.author: Myron
reviews.text: Interesting cave with good historical context. As many other reviews only came here as part of our cruise itinerary. Whilst cool enough I would not recommend the trip if staying on the island or not forced upon

reviews.author: Thomas Woods
reviews.text: Was a great experience, took around 45 mins to explore, this was with the help of a local guide who is on site, easy parking, tour guide waiting at the entrance was great and gave us some good history, definitely worth the money we paid and definitely worth a visit

reviews.author: Chris Clemens
reviews.text: Visiting Hospital Cave on Cat Ba Island was an unforgettable adventure! Full of history and hidden stories, this incredible site shows Vietnam’s resilience during wartime. Booking the tour with Full Moon Travel Asia made everything easy and insightful. Highly recommended for history lovers and adventure seekers!

reviews.author: Janusz Kurella
reviews.text: Amazing place. Hospital bunker built into a giant cave is an impressive example of war time struggles and challenges. For the 50.000 entry fee it’s definitely worth coming even tho it’s a short visit. Try admiring the bunker itself and its scenery instead of the „war artifacts” 10/10

reviews.author: Mike ORiordan
reviews.text: We had a guide from this cave site and it made a big difference in our appreciation of the facility, very interesting

reviews.author: Pedro Biaso
reviews.text: Nice to know. Is not that big, and don’t have many artifacts from the period. But definitely worth to go. Also the staff is very kind.

reviews.author: Ray Asyara
reviews.text: A smart move to build a hospital up above a mountain and in a cave to avoid detection fr enemies during the war. But how was it built during tht time.? Unbelivable !

reviews.author: Eva Persson
reviews.text: We had a guide that spoke very good English and told us all about the horrible war. Very interesting. Took 3 years to build and could hold 100 patients at a time. It was used for 20 years.

reviews.author: Max Poth
reviews.text: Interesting stop on the way to the National Park, it has 3 floors with the 3rd floor sadly being closed due to rockfall. You always enter with a guide who explains a little bit about the history. Takes around 20 minutes in total

reviews.author: Andrew Swinton
reviews.text: Pretty simple thing to visit, easy parking and ten to twenty minutes to walk through and have a look. You can swim over the road by the cafe, or 5km futher down the road - ask the guy you pay for directions

reviews.author: Jeroen (Yaron) Poppers
reviews.text: Interesting place, we didn't plan to visit here but was part of the Ha Long Bay cruise. Obviously interesting stories but the place itself is only a 20min visit and you've seen all.

reviews.author: Milan Marecek
reviews.text: Quite small. Way to experience hospital in cave, though. 80% of cave is conrete rooms and corridors so expect rather bunker than cave.

reviews.author: Paul Duce
reviews.text: Nice piece of history here. I had a personal tour guide who was quite knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Jenny Droppa
reviews.text: An interesting diversion. Fascinating to see this construction within a cave. It's well preserved as it's protected from most elements. The tour guide is friendly and helpful

reviews.author: Eugenijus Stratilatovas
reviews.text: Hard to evaluate. Its an impressive cave, but basically you see bunker of concrete. If to think its a historical sight then there is no information/narrative. Many tourist groups from cruise ships, so its rather full of people.

reviews.author: Robert Speller
reviews.text: Good place to visit on your way to or from the national park. Interesting to see the set up and how things operated. They do need a bit more signage to say what went on in each room but worth a look.

reviews.author: Ethan Bau
reviews.text: The structure is interesting, and I've never seen anything like it in a cave. However, it is quite short and lacks writing.

reviews.author: Julia Bachmanova
reviews.text: Very interesting experience. The tickets were 50.000 per person, and we were welcomed by a guide who gave us the brief intro to the cave. Definitely worth visiting if you’re in Cat Ba.

reviews.author: Alex Parlam
reviews.text: DONT MISS THIS PLACE, it’s absolutely worth it! Resilience of Vietnamese people is deeply inspiring!!

reviews.author: Kaylenn Cripps
reviews.text: I took my family to visit. 100k Dong per person to get a electric car from Cat Ba and back. Tickets can be bought at the carpark entrance. You climb a short serious of steep stairs, about 4 flights, to reach the cave. Lovely view of Cat Ba from the top. We had a fantastic tour guide, who walked with us through the complex at a leisurely pace, explaining the history as we went. Very happy to answer our questions along the way. An insightful experience for all. Well worth it.

reviews.author: Christopher Pagoulatos
reviews.text: It's a nice experience. If you have any move problems you have to know that you are about to have many stairs.
